document.title: Post Market Clinical Follow-Up Plan
standards: MDD 93/42/EEC as amended by 2007/47/EC, Annex X CLINICAL EVALUATION
MEDDEV 2.12/2 rev.2 POST MARKET CLINICAL FOLLOW-UP STUDIES
MEDDEV 2.7.1 & 2.7.2 Rev.3 Guidelines on Medical Devices (Clinical Evaluation)
MEDDEV 2.12-1 Rev.8 Guidelines on Medical Devices (Vigilance system)
EN ISO 14971[2012] Medical devices – Application of risk management to medical devices
MDCG 2020-7 Post-market clinical follow-up (PMCF) Plan Template - A guide for manufacturers and notified bodies

sourceSummary.oneLine: PMCF plan A-PMCF-001 Rev.1 for the ADD Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector under CE/MDD clinical evaluation and post-market follow-up requirements.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Intended for authorized competent authorities and notified bodies of H&abyz Co.,Ltd. and used to plan post-market clinical data collection after CE marking.
keyPoints: The PMCF objective is to confirm clinical performance and safety throughout the expected lifetime of the device and detect emerging risks.
The ADD is described as a wired or wireless digital flat panel detector for general radiographic diagnosis of adult and children, not for mammography.
The device is classified as Class IIa by Rule 16 of Annex IX, MDD 93/42/EEC as amended by Directive 2007/47/EEC.
The manufacturer plans PMCF activities through device registry and survey in the event of a serious accident on the product.
The plan references the Clinical Evaluation Report, Risk Management Plan, Risk Management Report, and FMEA Table.

concepts.name: PMCF objective
concepts.evidence: The objective is to confirm clinical performance and safety throughout the expected lifetime of the medical device, the acceptability of identified risks and to detect emerging risks on the basis of factual evidence.

concepts.name: CE-marked device clinical data collection
concepts.evidence: collect clinical data based on the use of a CE-marked device corresponding to a particular design dossier

concepts.name: Intended use
concepts.evidence: Digital Flat Panel X-Ray Detector is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children.

concepts.name: Contraindications
concepts.evidence: Not to be used for mammography and direct cardiac application. Not to be used for infant and pregnant women.

concepts.pageTarget: Equivalent Or Similar Device Clinical Data
concepts.evidence: Data from equivalent or similar devices may be used, for example to update the information relating to the state of the art, to identify and further assess relevant safety outcomes etc.

evidence.claim: The document is Post Market Clinical Follow-Up Plan A-PMCF-001 Rev.1 dated 2021.04.02.
evidence.evidenceText:
Post Market Clinical Follow-Up Plan
According to MEDDEV 2.12/2
Product Name Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector
Model Name ADD
File No. A-PMCF-001 (Rev.1)
evidence.pageTarget: Post Market Clinical Follow-Up Plan A-PMCF-001

evidence.claim: The product covered by the plan is the ADD Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector.
evidence.evidenceText:
Product name Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector
Model name ADD
evidence.pageTarget: ADD Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

evidence.claim: The PMCF plan is intended for competent authorities and notified bodies of H&abyz Co.,Ltd.
evidence.evidenceText: The information contained in this Post Market Clinical Follow-Up Plan is intended for authorized competent authorities and notified bodies of the manufacturer H&abyz Co.,Ltd.

evidence.claim: The PMCF objective is to confirm clinical performance and safety, acceptability of identified risks, and emerging risks throughout the device lifetime.
evidence.evidenceText: The objective is to confirm clinical performance and safety throughout the expected lifetime of the medical device, the acceptability of identified risks and to detect emerging risks on the basis of factual evidence.

evidence.claim: The plan scope is collection of ADD clinical data in the market after CE marking is acquired.
evidence.evidenceText: Collection of the clinical data of ADD in the market after CE marking is acquired.

evidence.claim: The plan states that clinical data will be collected according to the PMCF plan if a serious accident is reported on ADD.
evidence.evidenceText: If a serious accident is reported on our product, ADD, we plan to collect clinical data in accordance with this PMCF Plan later.

evidence.claim: The plan cites MDD, MEDDEV, EN ISO 14971, and MDCG PMCF plan template references.
evidence.evidenceText:
This report is made in accordance with the requirements
1) MDD 93/42/EEC as amended by 2007/47/EC, Annex X CLINICAL EVALUATION
2) MEDDEV 2.12/2 rev.2 POST MARKET CLINICAL FOLLOW-UP STUDIES
3) MEDDEV 2.7.1 & 2.7.2 Rev.3 Guidelines on Medical Devices (Clinical Evaluation)
4) MEDDEV 2.12-1 Rev.8 Guidelines on Medical Devices (Vigilance system)
5) EN ISO 14971[2012] Medical devices – Application of risk management to medical devices
6) MDCG 2020-7 Post-market clinical follow-up (PMCF) Plan Template - A guide for manufacturers and notified bodies

evidence.claim: H&abyz Co.,Ltd is listed as the manufacturer.
evidence.evidenceText:
Manufacturer contact details
Name H&abyz Co.,Ltd

evidence.claim: JaviTech e.K. is listed as the EU Representative.
ADD is intended for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy in adults and children and is not to be used for mammography.
evidence.evidenceText: ADD(Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector), Digital Flat Panel X-Ray Detector is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children. It is intended to replace film based radiographic diagnostic systems and provide more precise case diagnosis for physicians and radiologists. Not to be used for mammography.

evidence.claim: The intended population is adults and children except infants and pregnant women.
evidence.evidenceText: Intended Population Adult and children except infant and pregnant women

evidence.claim: The device contraindications include mammography, direct cardiac application, infants, and pregnant women.
evidence.evidenceText:
Not to be used for mammography and direct cardiac application.
Not to be used for infant and pregnant women.

evidence.claim: The target disease coding is ICD-10 R00-R99 for abnormal findings on diagnostic imaging and function studies, without diagnosis.
evidence.evidenceText:
Target disease
ICD-10 Code: R00-R99
Abnormal findings on diagnostic imaging and in function studies, without diagnosis

evidence.claim: The product class is Class IIa by Rule 16 of Annex IX under MDD 93/42/EEC as amended by Directive 2007/47/EEC.
evidence.evidenceText: Product class Class IIa by Rule 16 of Annex IX, MDD 93/42/EEC as amended by Directive 2007/47/EEC

evidence.claim: The manufacturer plans PMCF activities through device registry and survey in the event of a serious accident.
evidence.evidenceText: We plan to collect clinical data of post-market with PMCF activities through device registry and survey in the event of a serious accident on our product.

evidence.claim: The PMCF activities aim to confirm safety and performance, identify unknown side-effects, monitor side-effects and contraindications, analyze emergent risks, ensure benefit-risk acceptability, and identify misuse or off-label use.
evidence.evidenceText:
Aims of activities
- confirming the safety of the medical device
- confirming the performance of the medical device
- identifying previously unknown side-effects
- monitoring the identified side-effects and contraindications
- identifying and analysing emergent risks
- ensuring the continued acceptability of the benefit-risk ratio
- identifying possible systematic misuse or off-label use of the device

evidence.claim: Referenced technical documentation includes the Clinical Evaluation Report, Risk Management Plan, Risk Management Report, and FMEA Table.
evidence.evidenceText:
Clinical Evaluation Report (Doc.No. A-TCF-5-060 rev.1, 27.01.20
Risk Management Plan (Doc.No. A-TCF-5-036 rev.0, 10.04.2020)
Risk Management Report (Doc.No. A-TCF-5-037 rev.0, 15.06.2020)
FMEA Table (Doc.No. A-TCF-5-038 rev.0, 15.06.2020)

evidence.claim: The plan states PMCF evaluation will be conducted yearly after serious adverse events or accidents are reported.
evidence.evidenceText: We will conduct Post Market Clinical Follow Up evaluation yearly ever since the serious adverse events or accident of our product is reported.

reviewFlags.reason: Source metadata authoritySignal is FDA, but the source text identifies CE marking, MDD 93/42/EEC, competent authorities, and notified bodies rather than FDA authority.
Revision/date evidence is inconsistent: pages 1-12 show Rev. No. 1 Date 2021.04.02, while pages 13-14 show Rev. No. 0 Date 2021.01.28.
The PMCF activity summary table appears blank, and no specific activity number, endpoints, sample size, or timeline entries are provided.
The equivalent or similar device section provides template instructions but no named equivalent or similar devices.
